trans-unit.source: Exercise 3: Data sources
trans-unit.target: Exercise 3: Data sources

trans-unit.source: Characteristics of the exercise
trans-unit.target: Characteristics of the exercise

trans-unit.source: :::{card} :class-card: sd-text-justify __Aim of the exercise:__ ^^^
trans-unit.target: :::{card} :class-card: sd-text-justify __Aim of the exercise:__ ^^^

trans-unit.source: The aim of this exercise is to navigate various data sources, gain an understanding of where and how to access relevant data, and identify potential problems. It is important to **use reliable, up-to-date, and appropriate data sources** that fit the purpose of the analysis to ensure a successful and meaningful results. Always consider your analysis objectives and requirements and search for data accordingly.
trans-unit.target: The aim of this exercise is to navigate various data sources, gain an understanding of where and how to access relevant data, and identify potential problems. It is important to **use reliable, up-to-date, and appropriate data sources** that fit the purpose of the analysis to ensure a successful and meaningful results. Always consider your analysis objectives and requirements and search for data accordingly.

trans-unit.source: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card} __Larkana Flood Response Exercise Track__ ^^^
trans-unit.target: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card} __Larkana Flood Response Exercise Track__ ^^^

trans-unit.source: This exercise is part of the [Larkana Flood Response Exercise Track](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Exercise_tracks/en_larkana_flood_response.html).
trans-unit.target: This exercise is part of the [Larkana Flood Response Exercise Track](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Exercise_tracks/en_larkana_flood_response.html).

trans-unit.source: QGIS essentials
trans-unit.target: QGIS essentials

trans-unit.source: Finding and downloading relevant datasets and preparing them for further analysis
trans-unit.target: Finding and downloading relevant datasets and preparing them for further analysis

trans-unit.source: Data literacy
trans-unit.target: Data literacy

trans-unit.source: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card} __Estimated time demand for the exercise:__ ^^^
trans-unit.target: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card} __Estimated time demand for the exercise:__ ^^^

trans-unit.source: The exercise takes around 1 hour to complete, depending on the number of participants and their familiarity with computer systems.
trans-unit.target: The exercise takes around 1 hour to complete, depending on the number of participants and their familiarity with computer systems.

trans-unit.source: [QGIS Interface](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_interface_wiki.md)
trans-unit.target: [QGIS Interface](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_interface_wiki.md)

trans-unit.target: [Types of Geodata](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_geodata_types_wiki.md)

trans-unit.source: [Geodata Import in QGIS](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.md)
trans-unit.target: [Geodata Import in QGIS](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.md)

trans-unit.source: [Layer Concept](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_layer_concept_wiki.md)
trans-unit.target: [Layer Concept](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_layer_concept_wiki.md)

trans-unit.source: [Geodata Classification - Graduated](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_graduated_wiki.md)
trans-unit.target: [Geodata Classification - Graduated](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_graduated_wiki.md)

trans-unit.source: [Data Sources](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Module_2/en_data_sources.html)
trans-unit.target: [Data Sources](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Module_2/en_data_sources.html)

trans-unit.source: Instructions for the trainers
trans-unit.target: Instructions for the trainers

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} __Trainers Corner__
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} __Trainers Corner__

trans-unit.source: Prepare the training:
trans-unit.target: Prepare the training:

trans-unit.source: Take the time to familiarise yourself with the exercise and the provided material.
trans-unit.target: Take the time to familiarise yourself with the exercise and the provided material.

trans-unit.source: Prepare a white-board. It can be either a physical whiteboard, a flip-chart, or a digital whiteboard (e.g. Miro board) where the participants can add their findings and questions.
trans-unit.target: Prepare a white-board. It can be either a physical whiteboard, a flip-chart, or a digital whiteboard (e.g. Miro board) where the participants can add their findings and questions.

trans-unit.source: Before starting the exercise, make sure everybody has installed QGIS and has downloaded __and unzipped__ the data folder.
trans-unit.target: Before starting the exercise, make sure everybody has installed QGIS and has downloaded __and unzipped__ the data folder.

trans-unit.source: Check out [How to do trainings?](/content/Trainers_corner/en_how_to_training.md) for some general tips on training conduction
trans-unit.target: Check out [How to do trainings?](/content/Trainers_corner/en_how_to_training.md) for some general tips on training conduction

trans-unit.source: Conduct the training
trans-unit.target: Conduct the training

trans-unit.source: __Introduction:__
trans-unit.target: __Introduction:__

trans-unit.source: Introduce the idea and aim of the exercise.
trans-unit.target: Introduce the idea and aim of the exercise.

trans-unit.source: Provide the download link and make sure everybody has unzipped the folder before beginning the tasks.
trans-unit.target: Provide the download link and make sure everybody has unzipped the folder before beginning the tasks.

trans-unit.source: __Follow-along:__
trans-unit.target: __Follow-along:__

trans-unit.source: Show and explain each step yourself at least twice and slow enough so everybody can see what you are doing, and follow along in their own QGIS-project.
trans-unit.target: Show and explain each step yourself at least twice and slow enough so everybody can see what you are doing, and follow along in their own QGIS-project.

trans-unit.source: Make sure that everybody is following along and doing the steps themselves by periodically asking if anybody needs help or if everybody is still following.
trans-unit.target: Make sure that everybody is following along and doing the steps themselves by periodically asking if anybody needs help or if everybody is still following.

trans-unit.source: Be open and patient to every question or problem that might come up. Your participants are essentially multitasking by paying attention to your instructions and orienting themselves in their own QGIS-project.
trans-unit.target: Be open and patient to every question or problem that might come up. Your participants are essentially multitasking by paying attention to your instructions and orienting themselves in their own QGIS-project.

trans-unit.source: __Wrap up:__
trans-unit.target: __Wrap up:__

trans-unit.source: Leave time for any issues or questions concerning the tasks at the end of the exercise.
trans-unit.target: Leave time for any issues or questions concerning the tasks at the end of the exercise.

trans-unit.source: Leave some time for open questions.
trans-unit.target: Leave some time for open questions.

trans-unit.source: Exercise
trans-unit.target: Exercise

trans-unit.source: Available Data
trans-unit.target: Available Data

trans-unit.source: Download the data and project file for this exercise [here](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/Module_2/Backup/Module_2_Exercise_3_Data_Sources.zip) and unzip the folder.
trans-unit.target: Download the data and project file for this exercise [here](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/Module_2/Backup/Module_2_Exercise_3_Data_Sources.zip) and unzip the folder.

trans-unit.source: ::::{dropdown} Standard folder structure :::{figure} /fig/standard_folder_structure_new_2025.drawio.png name: standard_folder_struc width: 500 px
trans-unit.target: ::::{dropdown} Standard folder structure :::{figure} /fig/standard_folder_structure_new_2025.drawio.png name: standard_folder_struc width: 500 px

trans-unit.source: Standard folder structure. Source: HeiGIT ::: ::::
trans-unit.target: Standard folder structure. Source: HeiGIT ::: ::::

trans-unit.source: Task 1: Download the administrative boundaries and healthsites for Pakistan
trans-unit.target: Task 1: Download the administrative boundaries and healthsites for Pakistan

trans-unit.source: For our flood response map, we will need a few datasets from the web. In this exercise, we will be looking for the __administrative boundaries__ of Pakistan, the __healthsites__, as well as the __flood extent__ of the flood in Pakistan in 2024. First, let us set up a new QGIS project along with the standard folder structure:
trans-unit.target: For our flood response map, we will need a few datasets from the web. In this exercise, we will be looking for the __administrative boundaries__ of Pakistan, the __healthsites__, as well as the __flood extent__ of the flood in Pakistan in 2024. First, let us set up a new QGIS project along with the standard folder structure:

trans-unit.source: Download the folder structure and unzip it.
trans-unit.target: Download the folder structure and unzip it.

trans-unit.source: Create a copy of the folder structure and name the folder `module_2_exercise_3_data_sources`.
trans-unit.target: Create a copy of the folder structure and name the folder `module_2_exercise_3_data_sources`.

trans-unit.source: Open a new QGIS project and save it into the folder.
trans-unit.target: Open a new QGIS project and save it into the folder.

trans-unit.source: Now that we have the QGIS project set up, we can start looking for the datasets
trans-unit.target: Now that we have the QGIS project set up, we can start looking for the datasets

trans-unit.source: Find a data source to download the **administrative boundaries** and **healthsites** of Pakistan. The following instructions are designed for the example of Pakistan. If you wish to perform the same analysis for another country, some instructions may differ, but the general workflow will remain the same.
trans-unit.target: Find a data source to download the **administrative boundaries** and **healthsites** of Pakistan. The following instructions are designed for the example of Pakistan. If you wish to perform the same analysis for another country, some instructions may differ, but the general workflow will remain the same.

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} Possible data sources
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} Possible data sources

trans-unit.source: There are many different data repositories on the web where you can find suitable data. You can find a list of possible data sources [here](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Module_2/en_data_sources.html).
trans-unit.target: There are many different data repositories on the web where you can find suitable data. You can find a list of possible data sources [here](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Module_2/en_data_sources.html).

trans-unit.source: For most humanitarian data, you can search on the __[Humanitarian Data Exchange/HDX](https://data.humdata.org/)__ The Humanitarian Data Exchange (HDX) is a primary platform for accessing and sharing geospatial data relevant to humanitarian crises. It's a centralized repository offering a wide range of datasets from various sources, making it an invaluable resource for aid organizations and researchers.
trans-unit.target: For most humanitarian data, you can search on the __[Humanitarian Data Exchange/HDX](https://data.humdata.org/)__ The Humanitarian Data Exchange (HDX) is a primary platform for accessing and sharing geospatial data relevant to humanitarian crises. It's a centralized repository offering a wide range of datasets from various sources, making it an invaluable resource for aid organizations and researchers.

trans-unit.source: :::{admonition} Which data format to choose
trans-unit.target: :::{admonition} Which data format to choose

trans-unit.source: Most datasets on HDX are available in various dataformats such as xlsx, csv, shapefile, or GeoJSON. We are looking for spatial data that we can use in QGIS, so we will need a spatial data format such as `.shp` (Shapefile), `.gpkg` (GeoPackage), `.geojson` (GeoJSON), or `.gdb` (GeoDatabase)
trans-unit.target: Most datasets on HDX are available in various dataformats such as xlsx, csv, shapefile, or GeoJSON. We are looking for spatial data that we can use in QGIS, so we will need a spatial data format such as `.shp` (Shapefile), `.gpkg` (GeoPackage), `.geojson` (GeoJSON), or `.gdb` (GeoDatabase)

trans-unit.source: Download the data and save the **administrative boundaries** and the **healthsites** into the `data\input` folder.
trans-unit.target: Download the data and save the **administrative boundaries** and the **healthsites** into the `data\input` folder.

trans-unit.source: :::{Note} Make sure to only use the point data from the healthsites dataset. Other data shapes such as lines or polygons can be ignored in this example. Depending on the data source, information can be provided as points, but also as lines or polygons. :::
trans-unit.target: :::{Note} Make sure to only use the point data from the healthsites dataset. Other data shapes such as lines or polygons can be ignored in this example. Depending on the data source, information can be provided as points, but also as lines or polygons. :::

trans-unit.source: [Load both vector files into QGIS](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Module_2/en_qgis_geodata_concept.html#data-import).
trans-unit.target: [Load both vector files into QGIS](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Module_2/en_qgis_geodata_concept.html#data-import).

trans-unit.source: Now add the OpenStreetMap basemap via the browser window → `XYZ Tiles`. Adding basemaps can help you orient yourself, gain a better understanding of the area of interest, and create more informative maps.
trans-unit.target: Now add the OpenStreetMap basemap via the browser window → `XYZ Tiles`. Adding basemaps can help you orient yourself, gain a better understanding of the area of interest, and create more informative maps.

trans-unit.source: Familiarise yourself with the data by opening the attribute table and identify the different types of healthcare that are included in the dataset. Get an overview of the information that is stored in each column. For example, there could be information indicating the type of healthsite.
trans-unit.target: Familiarise yourself with the data by opening the attribute table and identify the different types of healthcare that are included in the dataset. Get an overview of the information that is stored in each column. For example, there could be information indicating the type of healthsite.

trans-unit.source: If your dataset contains information about the type of healthsite (e.g. clinic, hospital, doctor, etc.), we can extract these and save them in a new layer. We can do this by selecting the hospitals and then copying them to a new layer.
trans-unit.target: If your dataset contains information about the type of healthsite (e.g. clinic, hospital, doctor, etc.), we can extract these and save them in a new layer. We can do this by selecting the hospitals and then copying them to a new layer.

trans-unit.source: For information on how to easily filter your data by manually selecting features in the attribute table after it has been sorted based on a particular column, see the __[attribute table](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_attribute_table_wiki.md)__ page on the wiki.
trans-unit.target: For information on how to easily filter your data by manually selecting features in the attribute table after it has been sorted based on a particular column, see the __[attribute table](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_attribute_table_wiki.md)__ page on the wiki.

trans-unit.source: Task 2: Download the flood extent for Pakistan for August 2024
trans-unit.target: Task 2: Download the flood extent for Pakistan for August 2024

trans-unit.source: Now, let us download the flood extent for Pakistan from the 8 to 12 August 2024.
trans-unit.target: Now, let us download the flood extent for Pakistan from the 8 to 12 August 2024.

trans-unit.source: Go back to the humanitarian data exchange and search for __"Pakistan Flood"__. You will find a list of datasets containing the satellite detected water extents for different periods.
trans-unit.target: Go back to the humanitarian data exchange and search for __"Pakistan Flood"__. You will find a list of datasets containing the satellite detected water extents for different periods.

trans-unit.source: Choose the dataset with the title __"Satellite detected water extents from 08 to 12 August 2024 over Pakistan"__ and download the zip-folder.
trans-unit.target: Choose the dataset with the title __"Satellite detected water extents from 08 to 12 August 2024 over Pakistan"__ and download the zip-folder.

trans-unit.source: Unzip the folder and look at it's content. There are a few different shapefiles. We are looking for the __minimum flood extent__. Locate the files called `VIIRS_20240721_20240803_MinimumFloodExtent_PAK` and copy them to the `data\input`-folder.
trans-unit.target: Unzip the folder and look at it's content. There are a few different shapefiles. We are looking for the __minimum flood extent__. Locate the files called `VIIRS_20240721_20240803_MinimumFloodExtent_PAK` and copy them to the `data\input`-folder.

trans-unit.source: :::{admonition} Working with shapefiles :class: attention Shapefiles consist of several files (`.shp`, `.shx`, `.sbx`, `.sbn`, `.prj`, `cpg`). In order to copy the entire shapefile to the new location, __make sure to copy all the files with the exact name to the new folder__.
trans-unit.target: :::{admonition} Working with shapefiles :class: attention Shapefiles consist of several files (`.shp`, `.shx`, `.sbx`, `.sbn`, `.prj`, `cpg`). In order to copy the entire shapefile to the new location, __make sure to copy all the files with the exact name to the new folder__.
